title: Is Scituate native Conor Garland a worthwhile trade target for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Conor Garland, a forward with a $6 million cap hit, has been linked to Boston Bruins trade talks with Vancouver Canucks.
Garland, averaging 18.8 goals per season in the past four years, could strengthen Bruins' middle-six lineup.
Boston Bruins weigh trade options for Garland, considering team needs and the potential long-term impacts of his $36 million contract.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 4)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington saw a Colonial home selling for $500,000 at 1210 Washington St., built in 1950 on a 14,792 sq ft lot.
In Acton, a one-family Colonial at 7 Concetta Circle, built in 1988, sold for $1,175,000 on a 40,001 sq ft lot.
Boston's 100 Belvidere St. #8H condo sold for $3,700,000, featuring two bedrooms and three baths across 1,692 sq ft.

title: Patriots might have shot at top free-agent receiver after Colts’ franchise-tag gamble
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Colts opted not to franchise tag Alec Pierce, potentially making him a top free-agent target for the Patriots.
Alec Pierce led the NFL in yards per catch for the past two seasons, catching 47 passes for 1,003 yards in 2025.
To afford Pierce, the Patriots may need to restructure Stefon Diggs' contract or release him, freeing $16.3 million in cap space.

title: Vermont postal worker allegedly threw away mail she was supposed to deliver for months
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Natasha Morisseau, a Vermont postal worker, was cited for allegedly discarding mail in a dumpster in North Troy.
She faces nine counts of petty larceny and five counts of unlawful mischief, according to Vermont State Police.
Morisseau, suspended since Jan. 23, is scheduled for a court appearance on March 17 in Vermont Superior Court.

title: Man in his 80s, dog dead in Foxborough fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man in his 80s and a dog died in a house fire in Foxborough early Tuesday morning.
Foxborough firefighters, with help from Mansfield and Plainville, extinguished the fire on Granite Street within an hour.
The single-family home sustained major damage; the fire's cause is still under investigation by officials.

title: Patriots players brought strippers, hookahs to celebrate at a Boston restaurant, its owner says. He’s now facing a license violation.
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Estella's restaurant in Boston faces a license violation after hosting Patriots players with strippers and hookahs.
Restaurant owner Helder George Brandao blames players for the incident, which led to after-hours partying and noise complaints.
Boston Licensing Board may suspend or revoke Estella's liquor license; a decision will be made after Thursday's vote.

title: Mass. students place No. 1 nationwide for AP scores, break all-time record
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts students achieved the highest nationwide percentage of AP exam scores of 3 or higher, breaking a record.
35.8% of Massachusetts public high school graduates scored 3 or above on AP exams, per College Board results.
The state ranks third for AP exam participation and leads in Black or African American student participation besides Washington D.C.

title: ‘Forced to take this pay raise’: Quincy City Councilors are collecting the raise they promised to repeal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Quincy City Councilors are now collecting a 50% pay raise from $29,128.65 to $44,500, which they campaigned on repealing.
New councilors plan to donate their raises to non-profits, citing constraints by state laws and the city charter preventing immediate repeal.
Quincy's Mayor's pay raise was adjusted to $225,000, delayed to take effect in 2028 after drawing public criticism and council discussion.

title: Rosenthal: Red Sox remain the ‘best fit’ as a trade suitor for Isaac Paredes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox are considered a suitable trade partner for Isaac Paredes, despite acquiring Caleb Durbin for the infield.
A potential trade involving Jarren Duran could create designated hitter opportunities for Masataka Yoshida and Triston Casas.
Discussions between Red Sox and Astros have paused, though Paredes' right-handed power is appealing for Boston's lineup.

title: New ESPN mock draft has Patriots choosing a ‘pass-rushing menace’ in first round
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN's mock draft projects the Patriots selecting Texas A&M edge rusher Cashius Howell with the 31st overall pick.
Howell recorded 11.5 sacks last season, ranking seventh among FBS defensive linemen, and led the SEC in pressures.
Patriots seek to improve their pass rush, with New England posting just 35 sacks last season, sixth fewest in the NFL.
